metadata.title: MyHeic — Convert HEIC to JPG, free & offline
metadata.description: Convert HEIC files from Samsung & iPhone phones into JPG, right in your browser. Free, no login, offline-capable, EXIF preserved.
metadata.ogDescription: Free, no login, offline. Made in Indonesia.

hero.titleMain: HEIC photos from your phone, to JPG
hero.subtitle: Drop your HEIC files below — convert right in your browser, no install required.

converter.qualityTooltip: JPG is always larger than HEIC because HEIC's HEVC compression is more efficient. This slider only controls the JPG quality/size. 75–85 is recommended.

features.title: What makes MyHeic different
features.subtitle: Built because paid online converters at $25/year are frustrating.

noLogin.title: No login
noLogin.description: No account, no email, no verification. Open, drop a file, done.

free.title: Free forever
free.description: No conversion limits, no watermark, no subscription. Every feature is available without paying.

offline.title: 100% Offline
offline.description: Photos are never uploaded to a server. Conversion happens entirely in your browser/computer.

metadata.title: EXIF preserved
metadata.description: Original EXIF (datetime, GPS, camera, ISO, exposure) is carried into the output JPG. Your library stays organized.

simple.title: Built for everyone
simple.description: Indonesian & English UI, simple interface. Easy enough for non-tech family members at home.

desktopCta.title: Converting hundreds of files? Use the desktop version.
desktopCta.description: MyHeic for Windows — drag a folder, batch convert, save straight to the folder of your choice. Ideal for backing up photos from your phone.

items.q: Why does my phone save photos as HEIC instead of JPG?
items.a: HEIC is a modern compression format that produces ~50% smaller files at the same visual quality. Some Samsung phones and all modern iPhones use it when storage runs low or 'high efficiency' mode is on. Unfortunately, many laptops/PCs can't open HEIC without extra software.

items.q: Are my photos uploaded to a server?
items.a: No. Conversion happens 100% in your browser/computer — photos are never sent anywhere. The web version uses WebAssembly libheif running directly in your browser. The desktop version is fully offline.

The web version works on every OS (Mac, Linux, Chromebook, even mobile) — just open https://myheic.vercel.app in a modern browser. The desktop version is Windows-only for now because that's where HEIC issues are most common.

items.q: Why does Windows show a warning when I install the desktop version?
items.a: Because MyHeic isn't yet code-signed (signing certificates cost hundreds of dollars/year, and we chose to keep this free). Windows shows a SmartScreen warning for new software. Click 'More info' → 'Run anyway' to proceed. Or use the web version above — no install needed.

items.q: When should I use the desktop version?
items.a: Use the web version for quick 1–10 file conversions. Use the desktop version when you regularly convert hundreds of files, need batch save to a chosen folder, or work offline without a browser.
Yes. No conversion limits, no watermark, no subscription. If you find it useful, there's an optional 'Buy me a coffee' button — but it's entirely up to you.
